It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnosphaera rouhaniana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
